ud3.4:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud3.4:1.3:
And on that occasion Ven. Sāriputta was sitting not far from the Blessed One,
his legs crossed, his body held erect, having set mindfulness to the fore.
ud3.4:1.4:
The Blessed One saw Ven. Sāriputta sitting not far away, his legs crossed,
his body held erect, having set mindfulness to the fore.
ud3.4:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud3.4:3.3: so a monk whose delusion is ended
ud3.4:3.4: doesn’t quiver– just like a mountain.
